input.task: Vote on correction strategy for 43 domain contaminations

strategies.A_Surgical: Move ALL Experia logic to clients/experia/
strategies.B_Abstraction: Create generic interfaces, inject config per client
strategies.C_Hybrid: A for HIGH risk + B for MEDIUM + inline fix for LOW

reasoning: Each Council member evaluated the 3 strategies based on their expertise domain. Key insight from Pedro (Chair 6 / Domain Purity): squad generators are 100% client code and should be moved entirely (Strategy A), but self-correction.js uses domain terms to DETECT contamination — removing them would break the AP-001 check itself. This nuance made pure Surgical (A) unsafe and pure Abstraction (B) overkill. The Hybrid approach (C) correctly maps each risk tier to the appropriate strategy. Ng dissented favoring full abstraction for future scalability; Hassabis dissented favoring speed.
